brief.title: Gauntlet guarded-panel upgrade review
brief.summary: Review surface for executing the guarded-panel upgrade plus the v2 Gauntlet skill contract rewrite and follow-up eval/lint hardening: simplified modes, compact Release panel contract, TypeScript durability gate, behavioral skill evals, pre-commit skill-change checks, and deterministic checks.
brief.sinceLastReview: Replaced the review brief shell with a compact operating-log feed, additive snapshots, polished state transitions, and product-facing labels.

reviewItems.title: Confirm simplified mode model
reviewItems.why: The plan intentionally collapses the old mode matrix into Patch, Feature, Release plus Standard/Deep depth and triggered gates.
reviewItems.decisionNeeded: Confirm that removing Deep Patch and Slice as user-facing modes is acceptable for Gauntlet docs and skills.
reviewItems.agentNext: Keep terminology checks passing and avoid reintroducing old mode names except as explicit stale-string guards.

reviewItems.title: Review Release panel blocker bar
reviewItems.why: The panel guardrail is meant to reduce panel theater and blocker inflation.
reviewItems.decisionNeeded: Confirm the decision table and allowed decision vocabulary are the right contract for Release planning.
reviewItems.agentNext: Pressure-test the contract against Used Price planning outputs before considering the Do It worktree flow.

reviewItems.title: Check TypeScript durability gate default
reviewItems.why: The TypeScript standards should not slow lightweight UI work unless a concrete durability trigger exists.
reviewItems.decisionNeeded: Confirm the first classifier trigger set is broad enough for risky TS work and narrow enough for UI-only changes.
reviewItems.agentNext: Adjust classifier triggers only with evidence from real projects; do not turn durability into a global profile.

reviewItems.title: Validate Used Price pressure test
reviewItems.why: The plan requires comparing current and upgraded guarded-panel outputs against a realistic Used Price private-beta release scenario.
reviewItems.decisionNeeded: Confirm that the upgraded panel output is meaningfully better despite remaining classification variation on support and observability.
reviewItems.agentNext: Use the comparison doc as evidence before starting any Do It/worktree implementation flow.

reviewItems.title: Review v2 skill contract rewrite
reviewItems.why: The Gauntlet skills are the runtime contract agents follow; vague or oversized role skills create inconsistent orchestration, subagent output, and proof quality.
reviewItems.decisionNeeded: Confirm the suite should move as one v2.0 upgrade rather than selectively upgrading only the shorter skills.
reviewItems.agentNext: Keep future skill edits covered by the three-arm eval harness and require packet/report contracts for role skills.

reviewItems.title: Review skill eval and lint hardening
reviewItems.why: The v2 skills need fast regression checks for behavior shape, token budget, optional examples, and over-stretch defaults before future skill edits are installed globally.
reviewItems.decisionNeeded: Confirm that deterministic behavior fixtures are acceptable as scorer smoke tests and that live model response reps should be added as a later benchmark layer.
reviewItems.agentNext: When editing skills next, run lint-skills.py and run-skill-evals.py with real behavior response reps when available.

reviewItems.title: Confirm skill-change eval enforcement
reviewItems.why: The desired workflow is that any Gauntlet skill edit is checked before it can land, not merely when someone remembers to run the eval command.
reviewItems.decisionNeeded: Confirm commit-time enforcement is the right interpretation of 'every change' rather than a file watcher on every keystroke.
reviewItems.agentNext: Keep the hook installed in this repo and use GAUNTLET_SKIP_GIT_HOOKS=1 only for installs that must not touch git hooks.

reviewItems.title: Review brief operating log redesign
reviewItems.why: The review surface now starts with a compact feed of what changed, what needs the user, and a selected record, instead of exposing a checklist-heavy review model by default.
reviewItems.decisionNeeded: No human decision is needed unless the operating-log format feels too sparse for Feature or Release review.
reviewItems.agentNext: Keep snapshots compact and link deeper RB/CU/N/P records through the record trail when more context is useful.

changeUnits.title: Simplified Gauntlet mode model
changeUnits.reason: Replaces the old Patch/Deep Patch/Slice/Release user-facing matrix with Patch/Feature/Release modes, Standard/Deep depth, and triggered gates.

changeUnits.title: Guarded Release panel contract
changeUnits.reason: Adds the compact decision table, decision vocabulary, high blocker bar, panel delta, collapse rule, repeated-run synthesis, and review/triage preservation guidance.

changeUnits.title: TypeScript durability classifier
changeUnits.reason: Adds an executable gate that writes .gauntlet-ts-durability.json and defaults durability standards off unless concrete TS triggers exist.

changeUnits.title: Review brief freshness and data updates
changeUnits.reason: Carries forward the live review brief freshness support and updates this run's review data with meaningful records.

changeUnits.title: Used Price pressure test and naming decision
changeUnits.reason: Documents the Slice-to-Feature naming decision, keeps Release as the high-risk mode name, and records the Used Price planning/classifier pressure test.

changeUnits.title: Gauntlet v2 skill contract rewrite and eval gate
changeUnits.reason: Rewrites all Gauntlet role skills around packet/report contracts, bounded subagent use, cannot-verify slots, and concise proof-oriented outputs; adds a deterministic three-arm eval harness so future skill changes compare one-shot instructions, current skills, and proposed skills.

changeUnits.title: Skill eval behavior scorer, linter, examples, and stretch defaults
changeUnits.reason: Adds the requested v2 hardening: behavioral prompt/scorer support with no-guidance/one-shot/current/new arms, five-rep fixture smoke tests, prompt/output/speed metrics, a skill contract linter, optional cold examples, and explicit Not relevant because defaults.

changeUnits.title: Pre-commit skill-change eval enforcement
changeUnits.reason: Adds an idempotent git-hook installer and a skill-change check script so staged Gauntlet skill edits run skill evals and scoped skill linting before commit.

changeUnits.title: Compact operating-log review brief shell
changeUnits.reason: Replaces the old review/details/changelog shell with a feed plus selected-record layout, supports token-efficient snapshots, preserves sidecar freshness, and adds motion/focus polish without sample-data fallback.

notes.text: Gauntlet now treats Deep as depth, not a mode, and Feature replaces Slice as the user-facing product-work mode.

A Release panel is valid only when it produces a launch cut line, the Concern/Decision/Why Not Defer/Proof/Plan Delta table, and at least one concrete plan delta; otherwise it collapses to normal planner output.
The TS durability gate uses conservative path/config heuristics. It protects lightweight iteration speed, but should be pressure-tested against real project paths before expanding triggers.
Slice is renamed to Feature because it is clearer for user-facing product workflow work; Release stays Release because it names the operational risk posture better than Version.

Existing durable TypeScript patterns trigger durability standards for broad or non-UI durable work, but clearly UI-only changed files stay lightweight even in a repo that uses Zod or Effect elsewhere.

All 10 Gauntlet role skills should upgrade to v2.0 together because the shared packet/report contract is the improvement; keeping one role on the old style would break subagent handoffs and review-brief normalization.

Reviewer and tester skills intentionally grew because they now carry machine-checkable verdict, evidence, cannot-verify, risk, and agent-next slots; orchestrator and review-brief skills got shorter by moving repeated prose into shared contracts.
Behavioral eval support now scores response reps, but the bundled behavior-fixtures file is a deterministic scorer smoke test rather than a live LLM benchmark.

Optional examples live under each skill's examples directory and are loaded only when output shape is ambiguous; the hot SKILL.md path keeps only one pointer line plus the Not relevant because default.
Every Gauntlet skill change is enforced at pre-commit: staged `skills/*/SKILL.md` or `skills/*/examples/*` changes trigger `run-skill-evals.py` and scoped `lint-skills.py` before the commit proceeds.
The first screen uses additive snapshots so new compact summaries do not hide existing RB records; the shell now uses product-facing labels instead of explanatory captions.

proof.proves: The simplified mode model, guarded-panel contract, and TS durability classifier behavior are covered by deterministic checks.
proof.doesNotProve: Does not prove Used Price plan quality or all real-world TS trigger edge cases.

proof.proves: The review brief startup, sidecar freshness, file snapshot, and workflow instruction checks still pass after the upgrade.
proof.doesNotProve: Does not prove browser rendering beyond the existing freshness smoke from the prior pass.

proof.summary: Used Price pressure test compared one legacy guarded-panel plan with two upgraded guarded-panel runs using the same private-beta paid-launch prompt.
proof.proves: The upgraded panel contract produced a launch cut line, decision vocabulary, why-not-defer rationale, proof per row, and concrete panel deltas; it downgraded public-launch and rate-limit concerns instead of inflating every concern into a blocker.
proof.doesNotProve: Does not prove Used Price launch readiness or eliminate all nondeterminism; support and observability classification still require engineering-lead judgment.

proof.summary: Used Price TS durability classifier cases passed: src/components/UsedPriceApp.tsx returned durabilityRequired=false; src/lib/session.ts returned durabilityRequired=true with auth and existing durable-pattern triggers.
proof.proves: The classifier differentiates UI/lightweight UI work from auth/backend work in a real TypeScript repo that already uses Zod.
proof.doesNotProve: Does not prove every future path heuristic; it covers the current high-value UI and auth edge cases.

proof.summary: scripts/install.sh installed Gauntlet v2 to /Users/ajsathyan/.codex; installed check-gauntlet-workflow.py, check-review-brief.py, and run-skill-evals.py passed from the installed copy.
proof.proves: The global Codex copy has the upgraded workflow, skills, templates, scripts, eval assets, and deterministic review-brief checks.
proof.doesNotProve: The current running Codex session has reloaded the installed instructions; restart is still needed for a fresh session to pick up global changes.

proof.summary: scripts/run-skill-evals.py passed: every new_skill arm passed and every one_shot/current_skill arm failed across the v2 contract cases.
proof.proves: The proposed skill versions satisfy the deterministic v2 contract coverage that the baseline skills and one-shot prompts do not.
proof.doesNotProve: Does not prove all future LLM behavior; it checks explicit structural requirements and forbidden-pattern guards.

proof.summary: scripts/check-gauntlet-workflow.py passed, including the skill-eval comparison arm check and installed-layout support.
proof.proves: The Gauntlet workflow check now enforces that skill evals compare one_shot, current_skill, and new_skill and that installed copies can find the eval assets.
proof.doesNotProve: Does not prove semantic quality beyond the deterministic workflow and eval assertions.

proof.summary: docs/gauntlet-v2-skill-audit.md records the stretch audit, word-delta token proxy, and v2.0 decision for each rewritten skill.
proof.proves: Each skill was checked for overextension, token efficiency direction, and whether it should upgrade to v2.0.
proof.doesNotProve: Does not measure live model token usage or task success rates in production conversations.

proof.summary: python3 scripts/check-gauntlet-workflow.py passed in the repo and from /Users/ajsathyan/.codex/gauntlet with behavioral eval, skill linter, optional-example, and Not relevant because checks.
proof.proves: The workflow gate enforces behavior scorer coverage, skill contract linting, examples, N/A defaults, and installed-layout support.
proof.doesNotProve: Does not prove live LLM response quality; it proves deterministic gates and scorer plumbing.

proof.summary: scripts/run-skill-evals.py scored all new_skill coverage arms as passing and behavior fixture reps at 5/5 per case in repo and installed copies; scripts/lint-skills.py reported no failures for the scoped Gauntlet skill set.
proof.proves: The proposed skills satisfy coverage contracts, the behavior scorer path records five reps and metrics, and every scoped Gauntlet skill stays within the 500-word lint budget.
proof.doesNotProve: Does not replace model-run behavioral evals with fresh contexts and human-read scoring.

proof.summary: python3 scripts/check-gauntlet-workflow.py passed with the new pre-commit hook installer test; run-skill-change-checks.sh skipped README-only input and ran skill evals plus linter for skills/planner/SKILL.md input.
proof.proves: Gauntlet has deterministic coverage for installing the hook and for triggering eval/lint checks when skill files change.
proof.doesNotProve: Does not prove a developer cannot bypass git hooks with --no-verify.

proof.summary: validate-review-brief-data.py, check-review-brief.py, check-gauntlet-workflow.py, TS durability classification, stale-copy scans, and a Chrome smoke test passed after the operating-log redesign.
proof.proves: The redesigned shell loads real sidecar or embedded data, keeps freshness updates working without hard refresh, renders the feed and record trail, and avoids explanatory caption strings called out in review.
proof.doesNotProve: Does not prove every future generated review record will be well written; it proves the shell and deterministic guards for this redesign.

snapshots.title: Operating log review brief shipped
snapshots.doneSummary: The brief now opens to a concise feed, expands into a single selected record, keeps deeper evidence in the record trail, and uses product-facing labels throughout.
